category_title: Children with autism who exhibit disruptive or avoidant behaviors (shouting, random clicking) during difficult reading tasks
scenario_title: Formulating verbal responses during standardized reading assessments
minimal_context: The student is taking a standardized reading assessment with a familiar teacher and is asked to read a passage and retell the story.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Please read this passage out loud for the assessment.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Reads slowly) The boy was walking and talking.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Now, please tell me what happened in the story.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Long pause) Boy was walking and talking.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Can you formulate a different answer to tell me more?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Shouts) No! (Looks back at the page and reads with errors) Boy has... walking... talking.

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Please read this passage out loud for the assessment.

STUDENT: (Reads slowly) The boy was walking and talking.

TUTOR: Now, please tell me what happened in the story.

STUDENT: (Long pause) Boy was walking and talking.

TUTOR: Can you formulate a different answer to tell me more?

STUDENT: (Shouts) No! (Looks back at the page and reads with errors) Boy has... walking... talking.

context.environment: assessments were conducted as stated in the assessment manuals
context.materials: DORF and DRA assessment materials, tangible reinforcement

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: additional demand to formulate a verbal answer
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: making more errors with their reading as evidenced by word substitutions (e.g., reading “was ”as “has ”) and word omissions (e.g., leaving out small words such as conjunctions)
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: comprehension processing speeds were sometimes low

behavior_patterns.condition: For those with expressive language deficits (Sarah, with an average utterance length of 5 and Mark, with an average utterance length of 7)
